page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m8.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon processor operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from other tenants, though the lack of local storage and hardware acceleration limits its use to specific workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU positions this instance as a resource-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute parallelism. It is suited for small-scale databases, in-memory caching, lightweight enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to processing power.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m8.large is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. With dedicated CPU allocation and a high memory-to-core ratio, this server is designed for memory-optimized workloads that require consistent performance without high compute density. It is suitable for small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight enterprise applications.
